model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) holds a clear statistical advantage over Missouri, ranking 30th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +20.67 compared to Missouri's +15.36 at 52nd, with meaningful edges on both ends of the floor—particularly defensively where Miami's AdjD of 100.7 significantly outpaces Missouri's 104.1. The Hurricanes' 25-8 record in the ACC reflects a stronger body of work than Missouri's 20-12 in the SEC, and their roughly 5-point efficiency margin advantage is wider than the typical 7/10 seed gap. While the 7/10 line is historically one of the most upset-prone in the tournament and Missouri's SEC experience gives them a credible path, Miami's balanced superiority across offensive and defensive metrics makes them the deserving favorite in this matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Miami (FL) vs Missouri – West 7/10 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Miami (FL) is the stronger team by a meaningful margin across all major predictive metrics, with advantages in both offensive and defensive efficiency, a better record in a comparable power conference, and a roughly 5-point adjusted efficiency margin edge that is wider than the typical 7/10 seed differential.
evidence: KenPom: Miami 30th (+20.67 AdjEM, 121.4 AdjO, 100.7 AdjD) vs Missouri 52nd (+15.36 AdjEM, 119.5 AdjO, 104.1 AdjD)
Torvik: Miami 35th (121.1 AdjOE rank 35, 101.5 AdjDE rank 45) vs Missouri 46th (119.9 AdjOE rank 42, 103.0 AdjDE rank 68)
Miami holds defensive advantage of 3.4 points per 100 possessions in KenPom and offensive edge of 1.9 points
7/10 seed line historically produces upsets ~39% of the time, but this matchup features a wider quality gap than typical

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.

modelTrace.content:
The web search tools are currently unavailable, but I have strong ratings data to work with. Let me analyze the matchup based on what I have.

**Analysis Summary:**

| Metric | Miami (FL) - 7 seed | Missouri - 10 seed |
|--------|---------------------|---------------------|
| KenPom Rank | 30th | 52nd |
| Torvik Rank | 35th | 46th |
| Record | 25-8 | 20-12 |
| Conference | ACC | SEC |
| KenPom AdjEM | +20.67 | +15.36 |
| AdjO (KenPom) | 121.4 (34th) | 119.5 (50th) |
| AdjD (KenPom) | 100.7 | 104.1 |
| Torvik AdjOE | 121.1 (35th) | 119.9 (42nd) |
| Torvik AdjDE | 101.5 (45th) | 103.0 (68th) |

Miami holds a clear edge across all metrics — roughly a 5-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage in KenPom and a meaningful gap in Torvik as well. Miami's defense is notably stronger (45th vs 68th in Torvik, 100.7 vs 104.1 in KenPom), and they also have the offensive edge. The 25-8 record in the ACC is more impressive than Missouri's 20-12 in the SEC. The 7/10 line historically produces upsets about 39% of the time, but this matchup features a wider quality gap than typical 7/10 games.
